properties.remark: Several tree branches were blown down and power was out for about 6 hours.
REPORTED AT GBG.
Decayed tree split, powerlines down, and pieces of roofing material blown off a couple buildings. Timing estimated from radar.
Metal carport blown down. Time estimated from radar.
time estimated from radar. Public report of pieces of metal from farm outbuildings removed and a small area of a drip edge that was taken off a home.
Structural damage to a home porch.
Fence and a few ill-looking trees downed by thunderstorm winds. Time estimated from radar.
Large but decayed tree snapped near the base. Time estimated by radar.
Rotted tree snapped at its base at the Peoria Country Club Golf Course. Time estimated from radar.
A large section of sturdy fencing was knocked down across from the Kroger on Rt 40.
Roofing material ripped off of a large building. Time estimated from radar.
Restaurant sign blown over. Time estimated from radar.
A couple trees downed by thunderstorm winds. Time estimated from radar.
Trees down.
Tree down. Time estimated from radar.
60 to 70 mph winds estimated from New Berlin to Loami.
A grain bin was destroyed and part of a barn roof was damaged.
Large trees blown down on the south side of Washington.
Siding damage on outbuilding and garage.
Delayed Report. School bus barn roof was peeled off. Time estimated by radar.
Barn with 2nd level torn off, near Findlay along Rt 128. Delayed Report. Time estimated by radar.
Hail lasted for approximately 5 minutes and partially covered the road.
Trees down on north side of town.
Farm outbuilding destroyed.
Delayed report. Relayed from broadcast media. Time estimated by radar. Large 12 in tree limbs down on a house. Shingles peeled upward on roofing.

EF1, peak wind speed 110 mph. Path length 5.1 miles, width 150 yards. Most damage was to trees and power lines, but farm outbuildings were destroyed near the touchdown point, a well as county road 950E a quarter mile north of 2500N.
Barn lost a roof, approximately 1.5 miles east of the Hammond tornado track.
Gutters and roofing material ripped off of house, small outbuilding demolished, large limbs and cell tower blown down. Time estimated from radar.
Report from social media. Porch and portion of roof torn off of home.

Corrects previous tornado report from Hammond. EF2, peak wind speed 115 mph. Path length 12.8 miles, max width 150 yards. A farmstead lost over 2 dozen trees in far northern Moultrie County, with a home and several outbuildings also damaged. Numerous trees were damaged in Hammond, some of which caused building damage as they fell. The peak intensity occurred near the intersection of Don Ryan St. and county road 100, where an outbuilding was completely destroyed, though high winds also blew down a cell tower about 3/4 mile east.
Semis blown over on I-57.

*** 2 INJ *** EF2, peak wind speed 120 mph. Path length 11.3 miles, max width 300 yards. Touched down on the southeast side of Neoga, and damaged the roof of the high school and caved in the front door of the elementary school. Two injuries occurred in semis that were overturned on I-57 at the US-45 exit. The tornado tracked northeast and crossed into Coles County west of US-45, lifting along Lerna Rd. south of county road 450N.

EF2, peak wind speed 115 mph. Path length 10.1 miles, max width 150 yards. Numerous trees were damaged in Hammond, some of which caused building damage as they fell. The peak intensity occurred near the intersection of Don Ryan St. and county road 100, where an outbuilding was completely destroyed, though high winds also blew down a cell tower about 3/4 mile east.
Outbuilding destroyed. Time estimated from radar with a radar confirmed tornado.
EF1, peak wind speed 100 mph. Path length 0.6 miles, max width 75 yards. Snapped 11 power poles near the intersection of county roads 500E and 1100N.
time estimated from radar. Public photos shared of large tree branches broken and a small outbuilding shifted from the foundation.
power lines down. Time estimated from radar.
power poles and lines down. Time estimated from radar.
Fence down, roof damage, several trees and power lines down.
power poles broken and powerlines down.
15-20 foot spruce tree uprooted, fences blown down, road signs bent over along High Cross Rd. just north of Windsor Rd.
REPORTED AT CMI.
A tree was blown down near the intersection of S McKinley Avenue and W Church Street.

EF1, peak wind speed 100 mph. Path length 13.5 miles, max width 150 yards. Touched down just west of Sellars, mainly doing tree damage. Outbuilding damage occurred along county road 2000E just north of 2300N, with additional outbuildings damaged along 2600N west of 2100E. It continued north-northeast and went across the northwest edge of Gifford, causing shingle damage to several houses. It lifted in the Middle Fork Forest Preserve near county road 3300N.
time estimated from radar. image shared of an open-air farm outbuilding with walls collapsed and roofing blown off.
Machine shed collapsed.
Trees uprooted, structural damage to a trailer home, and a roof uplifted from a single residence home.

EF1, peak wind speed 90 miles. Path length 3.6 miles, max width 1/4 mile. Touched down near the intersection of E. 750th Rd. and N. 1400th St. It moved northeast across the southern part of Paris, damaging several homes, mobile homes, and trees. It dissipated on the north side of Sycamore Hills Country Club.
Delayed report. Relayed from social media. Time estimated by radar. Multiple power poles down east side of Paris along US 150.
a few trailers flipped.
Portion of barn roof removed, shingles on home blown off, and section of a large tree fell. Relayed be county EMA. Time estimated from radar.
Storm spotter looking WSW from Pekin at a tornado.
Trained storm spotter looking west at a tornado.
Corrects previous tornado report from Pekin. Storm spotter looking WSW from Pekin at a tornado.

A broadcast meteorologist video taped the EFU tornado touching down 3 miles ESE of Banner, just east of the Illinois River. The tornado moved northeast through grassy areas without creating any visible damage. It dissipated after about 1 minute.
Power pole bent and 12 inch tree snapped 2.5 miles NW of Delavan.
Three power poles blown down on Horse Barn Road. Ameren notified.
Trained spotter seeing a tornado approaching Mackinaw.
Power poles blown down. Ameren notified.
Large tree branches broken. Time estimated from radar.

Estimated wind gust along with dime size hail.
Some damage to outbuildings from strong thunderstorm wind gusts. Photos provided.
Semi-truck overturned by high winds at mile marker 7 on I-39 in median. Driver transported to hospital.
Tornado west of Comlara Park.
Large tree branch broken. Time estimated from radar.
Power pole blown down.
A power pole was snapped and leaning over, resulting in a detour on State Route 10 near Beason.
Power outages from strong winds and small hail.

EF1, peak wind speed 110 mph. Path length 0.7 miles, max width 50 yards. Structural damage was found near the start of the track, west of the intersection of county roads 1600N and 2000E. Here, a farm outbuilding was destroyed, and siding torn off a nearby house.
Large branch broken off.
Semi truck overturned on I-74 in Oakwood IL. Social media report with photo.

I-72 closed between mile markers 68 and 76 due to low visibility in blowing dust.

Photos of power poles down northwest of Mattoon on near highway 33.

The tornado moved from the SW corner of Champaign County to just north of Tolono. It covered 12 miles in 12 minutes doing EF1 damage to outbuildings and trees. It was 200 yds wide with a peak wind of 90 mph.
Morton Building collapsed at 1083 Caty Road 900 N 1.5 miles NNE of Tolono. Photos sent.
Storage unit damaged on county road 900 just east of highway 45 1.5 miles NNE of Tolono.
Building collapsed from high winds.
Several power poles and lines blown down west of 2.5 miles west of Charleston.
3 car crashes on southbound I-57 at mile marker 229 from high winds.
Pine tree broke off from high winds and landed on a car.

THIS CORRECTS THE START TIME OF THE TORNADO. EF0 STRENGTH WITH MAX WIND SPEEDS OF 85 MPH AND PATH LENGTH OF 12.79 MILES, TRACKED NORTHEAST FROM LOGAN COUNTY INTO MACON COUNTY AND ENDED IN DE WITT COUNTY. THE TORNADO SNAPPED A POWER POLE, DID TREE DAMAGE, AND THEN BENT OVER TWO SMALL METAL TOWERS BEFORE DISSIPATING JUST WEST OF US HWY 51 ALONG ROWELL ROAD.
Golf ball size hail in Kenney, photo from WCIA.
Quarter size hail in Kenney.
Egg size hail at 2000 Ave and highway 121 in Latham.
Semi truck blown over on NB I-55 at the Sherman exit. Time estimated by radar.

Downed trees from high winds in Downs. Roughly 4 to 6 inch diameter.
Report from mPING: Quarter (1.00 in.).
Flash flooding on Cook Street between Eastdale and Dirksen.
Power lines down from high winds with power out in Bellflower. Localized flooding.
Latham Fire estimating 60 to 70 mph wind gusts.
Latham fire estimating wind gusts to 90 mph.
An EF-U tornado was video taped progressing for about 1 mile to the northwest of Forsyth. No damage was noted.

EF0 STRENGTH WITH MAX WIND SPEEDS OF 85 MPH AND PATH LENGTH OF 12.79 MILES, TRACKED NORTHEAST FROM LOGAN COUNTY INTO MACON COUNTY AND ENDED IN DE WITT COUNTY. THE TORNADO SNAPPED A POWER POLE, DID TREE DAMAGE, AND THEN BENT OVER TWO SMALL METAL TOWERS BEFORE DISSIPATING JUST WEST OF US HWY 51 ALONG ROWELL ROAD.

EF0 strength with max wind speeds of 85 mph and path length of 12.79 miles, tracked northeast from Logan county into Macon county and ended in De Witt county. The tornado snapped a power pole, did tree damage, and then bent over two small metal towers before dissipating just west of US Hwy 51 along Rowell Road.
Power poles snapped 2 miles SW of DeLand.
Latham fire estimates wind gusts of 60 mph.
Outbuilding damaged east of Farmer City.
Powerline down at E Washington St and Argenta Rd between Maroa and Friends Creek.
Outbuildings damaged in Mansfield.

Tree damage in Mansfield.
Mesonet station CW2101 Mahomet.
Lots of standing water around Clinton. East Van Buren street shut down due to flooding under viaduct.
3 inch tree limbs broken by high winds.
Power pole snapped by high winds.
Two apartment buildings in Altamont lost their roofs at the A Frame Apartments.

EF1 strength, max wind speed 105 mph, path length 2.55 miles. Tracked northeast. Much of the structural damage was concentrated along N. 500th St. north of E. 700th Ave., where a home lost a portion of its roof.
Outbuilding blown onto road. Time estimated from radar.
Report from mPING: Street/road flooding.
Flood roads in Moccasin from heavy rainfall.
Large branch broken off tree.
Hangar damaged.
Corrects previous tstm wnd gst report from 2 W Louisville. Between 60 and 70 mph wind gusts on Kinmnudy Road. Corrected time.
Large Tree limbs blown down.
Tree blown down at 11461 N 1776 Rd.

EF1 strength, max wind speed 110 mph, path length 3.56 miles. Tracked northeast and reached peak intensity near the intersection of 2300th St and 1500th Ave, where it bent over 5 power poles. It appeared to lift just shy of the Jasper County line north of 1600th Ave.
Damage to small outbuildings and several large tree branches down.
Roof peeled off an animal pen.
Spotter reporting 60 to 70 mph wind gusts west of Louisville.
Power poles down.
The EF-0 tornado touched down about 1 mile south of Woodbury and was on the ground for 4 miles. It mainly damaged trees and several roofs of homes and outbuildings.
Outbuildings destroyed.
Tree and structure damage along I-70.

The tornado produced mainly EF-0 and EF-1 damage, but had periods of EF-2 damage (115 mph peak wind) where it snapped over 20 power poles and snapped numerous large trees. The tornado was on the ground for 44.62 miles in Illinois. The tornado crossed into Indiana for another 10.8 miles.
Tree limbs down, fence blown down, mature pine trees snapped in half.
Corrects time of previous tstm wnd dmg report from 2 N Greenup. Damage to small outbuildings and several large tree branches down.
Outbuilding destroyed and porch damaged. Time estimated from radar.
Tree damage in Greenup.
Three inch tree limbs broken by high winds.
Front porch removed from house. Barn and garage collapsed. Two sheds destroyed. Multiple trees down. Time estimated from radar.
Outbuilding destroyed and semi trailer overturned.
Outbuilding destroyed, and power poles broken.
Structure damage with barn damaged.

The EF-1 tornado touched down near I-70 and flipped two semi-trucks before it did significant damage to a hog farm. Debris from that hog farm was deposited over the full 8 miles of the track. Other damage during the tornado was to trees and a few power poles, along with outbuilding roofs.
Several buildings destroyed at a farm. Time estimated from radar.
Two semis overturned on I-70.
Barn destroyed along the county line at intersection of E. 2250th St. and Fern Lake Rd.
Tree blown onto a house. Time estimated from radar.
Between 60 and 70 mph wind gusts on Kinmnudy Road.
Report from mPING: Street/road flooding.
Portions of 15th, 12th, and James Street were flooded. 15th Street estimated 9 inches flowing water and road was closed in spots.
Carport damaged and tree limbs down.
delayed report. Large farm outbuilding had part of roofing removed and walls collapsed. time estimated from radar.
delayed report. social media photo of an uprooted tree. time estimated from radar.
Barns, trees, and power poles down from 2 miles north of Flora through north part of county.
Several power poles down near the intersection of Sailor Springs Rd. and Venus Rd.

Tree down on a power line along county road 750N.
The tornado touched down west of Victoria and tracked 16.48 miles north-northeast before dissipating north of Galva. It was on the ground for 18 minutes and rated EF-1.
The tornado touched down northeast of Altona and tracked 4.41 miles north before dissipating near Bishop Hill. It was on the ground for 7 minutes and was rated an EF-1.

The tornado affected mainly rural areas of Tazewell County where it produced EF-1 damage to trees, outbuildings and power poles, with peak winds of 90 mph. It reached a width of 100 yards as it damaged and uprooted numerous trees and snapped a couple of power poles. The tornado was on the ground for 9.5 miles before dissipating about 3 miles north of Deer Creek.
6 inch diameter tree limbs down, and about a 70 foot section of a barn wall was blown out.
Top of a grain bin blown off near the intersection of Washington and Robinson Roads.
Soffit came loose on a house.
Tree down blocking Old Highway 50 at Country Club Road.

Several videos of the tornado touching down just north of Effingham for about 1.5 miles. No damage reported.

Multiple photos and videos of a Landspout tornado in western Stark County west of Wyoming from broadcast media and social media. Location estimated. No damage reported.

Shared camera footage from on top of Memorial Stadium in Champaign looking due West of a dense, large wall of dust outside of town. Reports of zero visibility from this dust cloud.

Numerous tree branches and power lines were blown down across Decatur.

EF1, peak wind speed 90 mph, path length 10.3 miles, peak width 100 yards. Tornado traveled just south of the Crawford/Clark County line and crossed the border year West York. Much of the damage was near the Wabash River in York, to multiple trees and farm buildings. A local resident saw the tornado cross the Wabash River into Sullivan County, IN.
Time estimated from radar.
Measured gust, no damage noted.
Quarter to half dollar hail, relayed by media.
Largest stone measured 1.18 inches. Time estimated from radar.

Tree down on I-74 blocking eastbound right lane.

An EF-0 tornado touched down on the northwest side of Oakland and moved northeast. It damaged tree branches along Reel St and a tree grove to the northeast. It traveled 2.85 miles and lifted east of Walnut Point State Park.
Corrects previous funnel cloud report from Oakland. Report from earlier this afternoon. Several funnel cloud pics received, and video of a brief touchdown.

A spotter reported a rain-wrapped tornado south of Latham along 2300 Ave. No damage was reported. EF-U rating.

Rain wrapped tornado reported. Relayed by EMA.
relayed by media.

The tornado touched down about 1.5 miles southwest of Gerald along County Rd 2000 E where it did some tree damage. It then damaged an outbuilding along County Rd 2600, where it removed about a third of the roof and tossed it to the northeast. The tornado then dissipated a short time later. EF-0 rating with estimated peak winds 85 mph.
Video on social media.

The weak EF-0 tornado touched down about 4 miles southwest of Collison where it did tree damage. It moved east and was video taped by spotters as it passed about 2 miles southeast of Collison, where it did more tree damage. The tornado progressed northeast through the Horseshoe Bottom Nature Preserve then did additional tree damage along E 200 North Rd before dissipating.

The tornado touched down about 3 miles south of Armstrong along N 270 East Rd where it damaged some trees. It moved east and snapped two power poles along N 650 East Rd. I was then caught on video approaching Potomac Collison Rd before it snapped or uprooted around 12 large trees, roughly 2 foot diameter. The tornado dissipated shortly there-after to the east of that residence. EF-1 rating with estimated peak wind 90 mph.
Tree and power line damage along Potomac-Collison Rd. north of county road 2800N.
Video and photos of tornado.
A mile north of intersection of US-136 and IL-119.

The tornado touched down about 3 miles east of Bismark and produced some tree damage along E 2500 North Rd. It progressed to the northeast where it damaged the roof of an outbuilding before dissipating before reaching the Indiana State Line. EF-0 rating with estimated peak wind speed 75 mph.
Time estimated from radar.
1 to 2 foot diameter pine trees snapped.

Widespread tree damage on the south side of Westville due to downburst winds. Tree damage included snapped trunks, large branches snapped, trees uprooted. Minor roof damage noted on three homes. Wind damage continued approximately two miles east of town with additional tree damage and three power poles snapped. Wind speeds estimated at 60-100 mph.
A large tree was damaged.
Several roads flooded and impassable on the west side of Champaign including Interstate Dr, Mattis Ave, Country Fair Dr., and neighborhoods near I-57/I-72 interchange.

EF-U Tornado. Based on HiRes satellite imagery and a spotter report, it was determined a tornado briefly touched down in rural Scott County. It moved northeast and was on the ground in open fields for 2 minutes.
Approximately 6 inch diameter tree blown down. Photo shared via social media. Time estimated from radar.
EF-1 tornado with maximum winds of 100 mph. The tornado was on the ground for 0.7 miles and caused damage to large power poles, trees, and roofing material on a home.
Corn flattened in a narrow path near the water tower on Massey View Rd.
Large branches down.
Report of trees and power lines down in town.
Approximately 12 inch diameter tree snapped in Altamont. Photo shared via social media. Time estimated from radar.
Photo of pieces of sheet metal and insulation debris in a lawn. Tree limbs downed as well. Time estimated from radar.

EF-1 tornado with max winds of 100 mph. The tornado tracked 3.6 miles and was on the ground for 8 minutes. It damaged a power company outbuilding, a sports complex, numerous trees, and caused roofing damaged to a few residential homes.
Tornado reported on northwest side of Jacksonville.
Half of a large tree blown down onto a home.
Corrects location of previous tstm wnd dmg report from Effingham. Half of a large tree blown down onto a home in Sullivan.
EF-2 tornado with max winds of 125 mph. The tornado tracked 6.74 miles and was on the ground for 12 minutes. It damaged several trees, large power poles, a farmstead, and a couple houses.

An EF1 tornado with wind speeds up to 98 mph touched down along Allentown road about 3 miles east of Pekin High School and traveled about 1.75 miles along that road. It destroyed two outbuildings and snapped off numerous trees along Allentown Rd. It also snapped off 8 power poles along Springfield road north of the intersection with Allentown Rd. Some minor roof damage occurred east of Springfield Road as the tornado dissipated.
Power outage due to thunderstorm winds.
Several power poles snapped due to thunderstorm winds.
Barns destroyed. Possible tornado.

Storm chaser video shows a tornado approaching the Christian-Macon County line from the west, kicking up dirt and corn stubble as it does so. The tornado crossed the county line, continuing east-northeast into Macon County northwest of Blue Mound. As the tornado crossed the county line, it moved across power lines, but it did not damage them.

The EF-2 tornado touched down in far eastern Christian County where a spotter captured video of the tornado heading toward and crossing IL Route 48. The tornado remained weak with minimal damage until it crossed US Hwy 51 and reached Elwin. The damage between Elwin and Turpin included numerous snapped large trees as well as several homes with roof damage. Some of the more significant damage with this tornado occurred along E Skyline Drive as the tornado removed the majority of the roofs off of 8 homes and produced complete destruction of a garage that was tossed to the next block. Very large trees were also snapped off in that area. More home roof damage and tree damage occurred between S Baltimore Ave and S Mount Zion Rd, as the tornado crossed Bentonville Rd at Mount Zion Rd. The tornado weakened after that point, but was video taped by a couple of spotters progressing east all the way into Piatt County before dissipating near State Hwy 32. The tornado was on the ground for 34 minutes covering 23.6 miles with a peak wind speed of 120 mph.
Significant structural damage to home by a tornado.

An EF-1 tornado touched down about 3 miles east-southeast of Saybrook and destroyed a large outbuilding throwing the debris to the E- NE. The tornado appears to have dissipated before reaching the Ford County line. Radar images supported a brief tornado, with the circulation continuing into Ford County where it went on to produce a brief EF-0 tornado just south of Gibson City.
